name: llvm.x86.aadd32
description: APX AADD 32-bit: integer add that returns both the sum and computed flags in a GPR (doesn’t clobber EFLAGS); useful for big-integer add where you want carry/flags as data and to avoid flag dependencies.

name: llvm.x86.aadd64
description: APX AADD 64-bit: integer add that returns sum and flags in a GPR (no EFLAGS clobber); useful in multi-precision arithmetic and flag-heavy algorithms.

name: llvm.x86.aand32
description: APX AAND 32-bit: bitwise AND that returns result and flags in a GPR (no EFLAGS clobber); useful for tests/bitmask ops when you want ZF/SF/etc. as data.

name: llvm.x86.aand64
description: APX AAND 64-bit: bitwise AND that returns result and flags in a GPR (no EFLAGS clobber); useful for predicate creation and flag-driven control without EFLAGS pressure.

name: llvm.x86.addcarry.32
description: Adds two 32-bit ints with a carry-in and returns the sum plus carry-out; used for multi-precision addition, big integers, and crypto.

name: llvm.x86.addcarry.64
description: Adds two 64-bit ints with a carry-in and returns the sum plus carry-out; used in bignum libraries and high-performance multi-word arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.aesdec128kl
description: AES decryption of a 128-bit block using Intel Key Locker (key handle-based); used to implement AES-128 decrypt with protected keys.

name: llvm.x86.aesdec256kl
description: AES decryption of a 128-bit block using Key Locker with a 256-bit key handle; used for AES-256 decrypt with key protection.

name: llvm.x86.aesdecwide128kl
description: Parallel AES-128 decrypt using Key Locker over multiple blocks (wide form); used for high-throughput bulk decryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesdecwide256kl
description: Parallel AES-256 decrypt using Key Locker over multiple blocks (wide form); used for bulk decryption with protected 256-bit keys.

name: llvm.x86.aesenc128kl
description: AES encryption of a 128-bit block using Intel Key Locker (key handle-based); used to implement AES-128 encrypt securely and efficiently.

name: llvm.x86.aesenc256kl
description: AES encryption of a 128-bit block using Key Locker with a 256-bit key handle; used for AES-256 encrypt with key protection.

name: llvm.x86.aesencwide128kl
description: Parallel AES-128 encrypt using Key Locker over multiple blocks (wide form); used for high-throughput bulk encryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesencwide256kl
description: Parallel AES-256 encrypt using Key Locker over multiple blocks (wide form); used for bulk encryption with protected 256-bit keys.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesdec
description: One AES decryption round (InvSubBytes+InvShiftRows+InvMixColumns+AddRoundKey) on a 128-bit block; used in AES-NI decryption loops.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesdec.256
description: Same AES decryption round applied to two 128-bit blocks in a 256-bit YMM; used for parallel AES decryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesdec.512
description: Same AES decryption round applied to four 128-bit blocks in a 512-bit ZMM; used for wide, parallel AES decryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesdeclast
description: Final AES decryption round (no MixColumns) on a 128-bit block; used to finish AES-NI decryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesdeclast.256
description: Final AES decryption round on two 128-bit blocks in a 256-bit YMM; used in parallel AES decryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesdeclast.512
description: Final AES decryption round on four 128-bit blocks in a 512-bit ZMM; used in wide, parallel AES decryption.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesenc
description: AES-NI: one standard AES encryption round on a 128-bit block; used to implement AES encrypt loops.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesenc.256
description: AES encryption round applied per 128-bit lane across a 256-bit vector (two blocks in parallel); used for vectorized AES (ECB/CTR/GCM).

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesenc.512
description: AES encryption round applied per 128-bit lane across a 512-bit vector (four blocks in parallel); used for high-throughput parallel AES.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesenclast
description: AES-NI: final AES encryption round (no MixColumns) on a 128-bit block; used at the end of AES encrypt.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesenclast.256
description: Final AES encryption round per 128-bit lane across a 256-bit vector; used for parallel AES encrypt.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesenclast.512
description: Final AES encryption round per 128-bit lane across a 512-bit vector; used for wide parallel AES encrypt.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aesimc
description: AES inverse MixColumns on a 128-bit round key; used to derive decryption round keys from encryption keys.

name: llvm.x86.aesni.aeskeygenassist
description: AES key schedule helper (AESKEYGENASSIST) with round constant; used to generate AES round keys.

name: llvm.x86.aor32
description: Atomically ORs a 32-bit value into memory (LOCK OR); used for atomic flag/bitfield updates in multithreaded code.

name: llvm.x86.aor64
description: Atomically ORs a 64-bit value into memory (LOCK OR); used for atomic flag/bitfield updates in multithreaded code.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.add.cc
description: Atomic add that also produces x86 condition codes (flags); used when code needs flags from a locked add (e.g., multi-precision counters).

name: llvm.x86.atomic.and.cc
description: Atomic bitwise AND that also produces x86 condition codes; used by low-level runtimes that inspect flags after a locked AND.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.btc
description: Atomic Bit Test and Complement on memory with an immediate bit index; flips the bit and returns the old bit in CF; used for lock-free bitmap toggling.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.btc.rm
description: Atomic Bit Test and Complement with a register bit index; flips the targeted bit and returns the old bit; used for dynamic-index bitmaps.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.btr
description: Atomic Bit Test and Reset (clear) with an immediate bit index; clears the bit and returns the old bit; used for lock-free bit allocation/free.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.btr.rm
description: Atomic Bit Test and Reset with a register bit index; clears the targeted bit and returns the old bit; used for variable-index bitmaps.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.bts
description: Atomic Bit Test and Set with an immediate bit index; sets the bit and returns the old bit; used for test-and-set flags/locks.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.bts.rm
description: Atomic Bit Test and Set with a register bit index; sets the targeted bit and returns the old bit; used for dynamic bitsets.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.or.cc
description: Atomic bitwise OR that also produces x86 condition codes; used when flags (e.g., ZF) after a locked OR are needed.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.sub.cc
description: Atomic subtract that also produces x86 condition codes (CF for borrow, etc.); used in multi-precision arithmetic with atomics.

name: llvm.x86.atomic.xor.cc
description: Atomically XORs a value into memory (LOCKed read‑modify‑write) and returns condition‑code info (e.g., zero flag); used for lock‑free bitset updates and low‑level sync that needs flag feedback.

name: llvm.x86.avx.addsub.pd.256
description: Alternating add/subtract on packed 64‑bit floats across 256 bits; useful for complex arithmetic (real±imag) and DSP kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx.addsub.ps.256
description: Alternating add/subtract on packed 32‑bit floats across 256 bits; used in complex‑number ops and signal processing loops.

name: llvm.x86.avx.blendv.pd.256
description: Per‑lane select between two packed doubles using a vector mask’s sign bits; for masked moves and vectorized conditionals.

name: llvm.x86.avx.blendv.ps.256
description: Per‑lane select between two packed floats using a vector mask’s sign bits; used for if‑else selection and masked updates.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cmp.pd.256
description: Compare packed doubles per predicate (imm8), producing an all‑ones/zero mask per lane; used for vector masks and branching.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cmp.ps.256
description: Compare packed floats per predicate (imm8), yielding a per‑lane mask; used for vector conditionals and masking.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cvt.pd2.ps.256
description: Convert packed double‑precision to single‑precision (narrowing 64‑bit to 32‑bit floats); used when downshifting precision or interfacing with float code.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cvt.pd2dq.256
description: Convert packed doubles to signed 32‑bit ints using current MXCSR rounding mode; used for quantization, indexing, and storing to int arrays.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cvt.ps2dq.256
description: Convert packed floats to signed 32‑bit ints using current MXCSR rounding mode; used for index generation and numeric conversion.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cvtt.pd2dq.256
description: Convert packed doubles to signed 32‑bit ints with truncation (toward zero); used for C‑style cast semantics and fast truncation.

name: llvm.x86.avx.cvtt.ps2dq.256
description: Convert packed floats to signed 32‑bit ints with truncation (toward zero); used for fast float‑to‑int casts without rounding.

name: llvm.x86.avx.dp.ps.256
description: Dot product of packed floats with imm8 controlling which terms accumulate and where results are written; used in linear algebra and graphics.

name: llvm.x86.avx.hadd.pd.256
description: Horizontal add of packed doubles within each 128‑bit lane of a 256‑bit vector; used for pairwise sums and partial reductions.

name: llvm.x86.avx.hadd.ps.256
description: Horizontal add of packed floats within 128‑bit lanes; used to reduce adjacent elements efficiently.

name: llvm.x86.avx.hsub.pd.256
description: Horizontal subtract of packed doubles within 128‑bit lanes; used for pairwise differences and spectral transforms.

name: llvm.x86.avx.hsub.ps.256
description: Horizontal subtract of packed floats within 128‑bit lanes; used for adjacent differences and transform kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx.ldu.dq.256
description: Unaligned 256‑bit load (VLD[D]QU) optimized for possibly misaligned memory; used when reading data with unknown or non‑aligned addresses.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskload.pd
description: Masked load of packed doubles (128‑bit): load lanes where mask MSB is set, zero others; used for tail handling and bounds‑safe loads.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskload.pd.256
description: Masked load of packed doubles (256‑bit): load lanes where mask MSB is set, zero others; used for masked tails and edge handling in loops.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskload.ps
description: Masked load of 4 floats; loads lanes with mask sign bit set, others zero; used for safe tail/remainder loads.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskload.ps.256
description: Masked load of 8 floats; loads only masked lanes, others zero; used for boundary-safe SIMD and partial vectors.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskstore.pd
description: Masked store of 2 doubles; writes only lanes with mask sign bit set; used for conditional/tail stores without branching.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskstore.pd.256
description: Masked store of 4 doubles; stores only masked lanes; used for remainder handling and conditional updates.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskstore.ps
description: Masked store of 4 floats; writes only selected lanes; used for conditional stores and vector tails.

name: llvm.x86.avx.maskstore.ps.256
description: Masked store of 8 floats; selective lane writes; useful for masked updates and loop epilogues.

name: llvm.x86.avx.max.pd.256
description: Pairwise max of 4 doubles; used for clamps, thresholds, and vectorized max operations.

name: llvm.x86.avx.max.ps.256
description: Pairwise max of 8 floats; used for clipping, activation functions, and elementwise maxima.

name: llvm.x86.avx.min.pd.256
description: Pairwise min of 4 doubles; used for clamps, thresholds, and vectorized min operations.

name: llvm.x86.avx.min.ps.256
description: Pairwise min of 8 floats; used for clipping and elementwise minima.

name: llvm.x86.avx.movmsk.pd.256
description: Extracts sign bits of 4 doubles to a 4-bit mask; used to summarize compares or sign tests.

name: llvm.x86.avx.movmsk.ps.256
description: Extracts sign bits of 8 floats to an 8-bit mask; used for any/all tests and branching.

name: llvm.x86.avx.ptestc.256
description: Bit test: (~a & b) == 0 (b ⊆ a); used for subset/containment mask checks.

name: llvm.x86.avx.ptestnzc.256
description: Bit test: (a & b) != 0 and (~a & b) != 0; used for partial-overlap mask detection.

name: llvm.x86.avx.ptestz.256
description: Bit test: (a & b) == 0 (disjoint); used for fast “any overlap?” checks.

name: llvm.x86.avx.rcp.ps.256
description: Approximate reciprocal of 8 floats; fast 1/x (often refined); used in graphics and normalization.

name: llvm.x86.avx.round.pd.256
description: Rounds 4 doubles per mode imm (nearest/floor/ceil/trunc) without changing FP state; used for quantization/indexing.

name: llvm.x86.avx.round.ps.256
description: Rounds 8 floats per mode imm without FP state changes; used for vectorized rounding and binning.

name: llvm.x86.avx.rsqrt.ps.256
description: Approximate reciprocal square root of 8 floats; fast 1/sqrt(x) (refinable); used for normalization and lighting.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vpermilvar.pd
description: Variable permute of doubles within 128-bit lanes using per-element indices; used for swizzles, broadcasts, and transposes.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vpermilvar.pd.256
description: Variable permute of packed doubles within each 128-bit lane of a 256-bit vector using a control vector; used for swizzles, in-lane transposes, and SIMD data reordering.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vpermilvar.ps
description: Variable permute of packed floats within a 128-bit vector using a control vector; used for swizzles, channel reorders, and small transposes.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vpermilvar.ps.256
description: Variable permute of packed floats within each 128-bit lane of a 256-bit vector using a control vector; used for swizzles and in-lane transposes in vector math.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestc.pd
description: Bitwise test “subset”: returns true if (a & ~b) == 0 on packed doubles; used to check if one mask is contained in another before blends/masks.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestc.pd.256
description: 256-bit version of bitwise test “subset” for packed doubles; used for mask-inclusion checks across YMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestc.ps
description: Bitwise test “subset”: returns true if (a & ~b) == 0 on packed floats; used for mask checks before conditional ops.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestc.ps.256
description: 256-bit version of bitwise test “subset” for packed floats; used to quickly test if one mask is contained in another.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestnzc.pd
description: Bitwise test “nonzero and noncarry”: returns true if (a & b) != 0 and (a & ~b) != 0 on packed doubles; used to detect partial overlap between masks.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestnzc.pd.256
description: 256-bit version of “nonzero and noncarry” test for packed doubles; used to detect partial mask overlap across YMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestnzc.ps
description: Bitwise test “nonzero and noncarry” on packed floats; returns true if masks partially overlap; useful for fast mask-flow control.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestnzc.ps.256
description: 256-bit version of “nonzero and noncarry” test on packed floats; used to detect partial overlap of YMM masks.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestz.pd
description: Bitwise test “zero”: returns true if (a & b) == 0 on packed doubles; used to check if no lanes are active under a mask.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestz.pd.256
description: 256-bit version of “zero” test for packed doubles; used for fast early-out when a masked operation has no active lanes.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestz.ps
description: Bitwise test “zero” on packed floats; returns true if (a & b) == 0; used for mask emptiness checks.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vtestz.ps.256
description: 256-bit version of “zero” test on packed floats; used to skip work when YMM mask has no active lanes.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vzeroall
description: Zero all YMM/XMM registers; used to break false dependencies and avoid AVX-to-SSE transition penalties or to sanitize vector state.

name: llvm.x86.avx.vzeroupper
description: Zero upper 128 bits of all YMM registers; used when returning to SSE/legacy code to avoid AVX-to-SSE transition penalties.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.d
description: Gather 32-bit integers into a 128-bit vector from non-contiguous addresses base + index[i]*scale with mask/pass-through; used to vectorize indirect loads and sparse access patterns.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.d.256
description: Gather 32-bit integers into a 256-bit vector (8 lanes) from base + index[i]*scale with mask/pass-through; used for vectorizing irregular memory accesses.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.pd
description: Gather double-precision floats using 32-bit indices into a 128-bit vector (2 lanes) from base + index[i]*scale with mask/pass-through; used for vectorizing indirect FP loads.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.pd.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of doubles into YMM using 32-bit indices and scale (base + idx*scale); for sparse/indirect loads, LUT lookups, SpMV.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.ps
description: AVX2 masked gather of floats into XMM using 32-bit indices and scale; for irregular vector loads, table lookups, sparse/graph kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.ps.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of floats into YMM using 32-bit indices and scale; for sparse/indirect memory access and LUTs.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.q
description: AVX2 masked gather of 64-bit integers into XMM using 32-bit indices and scale; for indirect integer array reads in SIMD loops.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.d.q.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of 64-bit integers into YMM using 32-bit indices and scale; for sparse integer data access.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.d
description: AVX2 masked gather of 32-bit integers into XMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for indirect reads with large index space.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.d.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of 32-bit integers into YMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for sparse/graph workloads with 64-bit indices.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.pd
description: AVX2 masked gather of doubles into XMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for indirect double loads with large address spaces.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.pd.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of doubles into YMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for sparse/indirect double-precision access.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.ps
description: AVX2 masked gather of floats into XMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for irregular float loads with 64-bit indices.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.ps.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of floats into YMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for sparse/indirect float access.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.q
description: AVX2 masked gather of 64-bit integers into XMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for indirect 64-bit integer loads in sparse/graph code.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.gather.q.q.256
description: AVX2 masked gather of 64-bit integers into YMM using 64-bit indices and scale; for sparse integer data access with 64-bit indices.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskload.d
description: AVX2 masked load of 32-bit elements into XMM; only lanes enabled by mask are loaded; for conditional/tail loads and irregular reads.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskload.d.256
description: AVX2 masked load of 32-bit elements into YMM; loads lanes selected by mask; for tails, bounds-safe SIMD loads.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskload.q
description: AVX2 masked load of 64-bit elements into XMM; loads only masked lanes; for conditional/tail 64-bit loads.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskload.q.256
description: AVX2 masked load of 64-bit elements into YMM; masked lanes only; for partial vectors and irregular access.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskstore.d
description: AVX2 masked store of 32-bit elements from XMM to memory; writes only masked lanes; for conditional/tail stores.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskstore.d.256
description: AVX2 masked store of 32-bit elements from YMM; writes selected lanes only; for tails and predicated stores.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskstore.q
description: AVX2 masked store of 64-bit elements from XMM; writes only masked lanes; for conditional/tail 64-bit stores.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.maskstore.q.256
description: Conditionally stores 64-bit elements from a 256-bit vector to memory where the corresponding mask element’s MSB is set; used for masked/tail stores, predicated writes, and sparse updates.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.mpsadbw
description: Computes multiple 4-byte sums of absolute differences between two byte vectors, producing eight 16-bit SADs per 128-bit lane; used in motion estimation, block matching, and template/string matching.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.packssdw
description: Packs two vectors of 32-bit signed ints into 16-bit signed with saturation; used to narrow/clamp 32-bit results (e.g., filters, transforms) back to int16.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.packsswb
description: Packs two vectors of 16-bit signed ints into 8-bit signed with saturation; used for narrowing/clamping to int8 (pixels, audio).

name: llvm.x86.avx2.packusdw
description: Packs two vectors of 32-bit signed ints into 16-bit unsigned with saturation to [0,65535]; used for narrowing to uint16 in image/video pipelines.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.packuswb
description: Packs two vectors of 16-bit signed ints into 8-bit unsigned with saturation to [0,255]; used for clamping to 8-bit pixels/bytes.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pavg.b
description: Computes rounded average of unsigned bytes ((a+b+1)>>1); used in image blending, scaling, and denoising.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pavg.w
description: Computes rounded average of unsigned 16-bit words; used in image/video blending and filters.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pblendvb
description: Per-byte variable blend between two vectors using mask MSBs to select elements; used for conditional merges, thresholding, and simple selection/alpha-like operations.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.permd
description: Permutes 32-bit integer elements according to an index vector; used for swizzles, table lookups, and data rearrangements.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.permps
description: Permutes 32-bit float elements according to an index vector; used for swizzles and data reordering in shuffle-heavy kernels (e.g., FFTs).

name: llvm.x86.avx2.phadd.d
description: Horizontal add of adjacent 32-bit ints within lanes for two inputs (a0+a1, a2+a3, … then b0+b1, …); used for pairwise sums and partial reductions.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.phadd.sw
description: Horizontal add of adjacent signed 16-bit ints with saturation; used for fixed-point sums where overflow must clamp (audio mixing, filters).

name: llvm.x86.avx2.phadd.w
description: Horizontal add of adjacent 16-bit ints (wraparound); used for pairwise sums/reductions when saturation isn’t needed.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.phsub.d
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent 32-bit ints within lanes (a0−a1, a2−a3, … then b0−b1, …); used in gradient/difference computations.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.phsub.sw
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent signed 16-bit ints with saturation; used for bounded difference filters in fixed-point image/audio processing.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.phsub.w
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent 16-bit ints (wraparound); used for edge/gradient and difference stages where saturation isn’t required.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pmadd.ub.sw
description: Multiply unsigned bytes by signed bytes pairwise, then horizontally add with signed saturation to 16-bit (PMADDUBSW); used for int8 dot products, color conversion, and small-kernel convolutions.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pmadd.wd
description: Multiply 16-bit signed words to 32-bit and horizontally add adjacent pairs (PMADDWD); used for int16 MACs, FIR filters, and correlation.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pmovmskb
description: Extracts the MSB of each byte into a bitmask integer; used to compress compare results for branching, scanning (memchr/memcmp), and index/mask generation.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pmul.hr.sw
description: Multiply signed 16-bit pairs, add 0x8000, shift right 15, pack with signed saturation; ideal for Q15 fixed‑point multiplies with rounding in audio/DSP.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pmulh.w
description: Multiply signed 16‑bit pairs and return the high 16 bits of each 32‑bit product; used for fixed‑point scaling (Q‑format) and fast approximate products.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pmulhu.w
description: Multiply unsigned 16‑bit pairs and return the high 16 bits of each 32‑bit product; used for unsigned fixed‑point arithmetic and image processing.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psad.bw
description: Compute per‑lane sums of absolute differences of unsigned bytes over fixed 8‑byte groups; used for SAD metrics in motion estimation, block matching, and image/video codecs.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pshuf.b
description: Shuffle bytes within each 128‑bit lane by a control mask (high control bit zeros the output); used for table lookups, byte permutes, endian swaps, and packing/unpacking.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psign.b
description: Conditional sign/zero: per byte, negate if control is negative, zero if control is zero, otherwise pass through; used for applying sign masks, abs/negation, and SIMD math kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psign.d
description: Conditional sign/zero on 32‑bit elements based on control signs; used for dot products, FFT twiddle signs, and branchless conditional negation.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psign.w
description: Conditional sign/zero on 16‑bit elements based on control signs; used in DSP, audio, and saturating math patterns.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psll.d
description: Logical left shift of packed 32‑bit ints by a count from a vector (same count per 128‑bit lane); used to multiply by powers of two, bitfield packing, and mask generation.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psll.q
description: Logical left shift of packed 64‑bit ints by a lane‑wide count from a vector; used for 64‑bit bitfield ops, hashing, and crypto bit twiddling.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psll.w
description: Logical left shift of packed 16‑bit ints by a lane‑wide count from a vector; used for fixed‑point scaling and mask construction.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pslli.d
description: Logical left shift of packed 32‑bit ints by an immediate count (same for all elements); used when the shift is a compile‑time constant.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pslli.q
description: Logical left shift of packed 64‑bit ints by an immediate count; used for 64‑bit constant‑count bit manipulation.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.pslli.w
description: Logical left shift of packed 16‑bit ints by an immediate count; used for constant‑count fixed‑point scaling and masking.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psllv.d
description: Per‑element variable logical left shift of 32‑bit ints by counts from another vector (128‑bit lane); used for data‑dependent bit twiddling, mixes, and permutes.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psllv.d.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psllv.q

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psllv.q.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psra.d

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psra.w

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrai.d

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrai.w

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrav.d

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrav.d.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrl.d

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrl.q

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrl.w

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrli.d

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrli.q

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrli.w

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrlv.d
description: Per-lane variable logical right shift of packed 32-bit integers (128-bit); used when each element has its own unsigned shift.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrlv.d.256
description: Per-lane variable logical right shift of packed 32-bit integers (256-bit); elementwise unsigned scaling/bit ops.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrlv.q

name: llvm.x86.avx2.psrlv.q.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbssd.128

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbssd.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbssds.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbsud.128

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbsud.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbsuds.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbuuds.128

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpbuuds.256

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwsuds.128

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwusd.128

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwusd.256
description: 256-bit word dot-product: per 32-bit lane sum of 2 (uint16×int16) products, non-saturating add into 32-bit accumulators; used in mixed-sign int16 GEMM/conv on YMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwusds.128
description: 128-bit word dot-product: per 32-bit lane sum of 2 (uint16×int16) products, added with signed saturation; for robust mixed-sign int16 accumulations on XMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwusds.256
description: 256-bit word dot-product: per 32-bit lane sum of 2 (uint16×int16) products, added with signed saturation; for robust mixed-sign int16 accumulations on YMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwuud.128
description: 128-bit word dot-product: per 32-bit lane sum of 2 (uint16×uint16) products, non-saturating add into 32-bit accumulators; int16 GEMM/filters on XMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwuud.256
description: 256-bit word dot-product: per 32-bit lane sum of 2 (uint16×uint16) products, non-saturating add into 32-bit accumulators; int16 GEMM/filters on YMM.

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwuuds.128

name: llvm.x86.avx2.vpdpwuuds.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512.add.pd.512

name: llvm.x86.avx512.add.ps.512

name: llvm.x86.avx512.broadcastmb.128

name: llvm.x86.avx512.broadcastmb.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512.broadcastmw.128

name: llvm.x86.avx512.conflict.q.512

name: llvm.x86.avx512.mask.cvtpd2udq.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512.mask.cvtpd2uqq.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2uqq.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2uqq.512

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2uw.128

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2uw.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2uw.512
description: Masked vector convert FP16 to unsigned 16-bit integers, 512-bit; uses current rounding; for quantizing to u16 (image/video pixels, compact storage).

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2w.128

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2w.256
description: Masked vector convert FP16 to signed 16-bit integers, 256-bit; uses current rounding; for quantizing to i16 (audio/DSP data, compact storage).

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtph2w.512
description: Masked vector convert FP16 to signed 16-bit integers, 512-bit; uses current rounding; for quantizing to i16 (audio/DSP data, compact storage).

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtps2phx.512

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtqq2ph.128

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtqq2ph.256

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtsd2sh.round

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtsh2sd.round

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtsh2ss.round
description: Convert scalar half to single with specified rounding/SAE and masking; used to ingest FP16 data into FP32 computations.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtss2sh.round
description: Convert scalar single to half with specified rounding/SAE and masking; used to narrow FP32 scalars for storage or mixed-precision compute.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2dq.128
description: Convert packed float16 to signed int32 with truncation (toward zero) and masking (128-bit); used for dequantization to ints or parsing FP16 to integers.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2dq.256
description: Convert packed float16 to signed int32 with truncation and masking (256-bit); useful in vectorized FP16→int32 conversion.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2dq.512
description: Convert packed float16 to signed int32 with truncation and masking (512-bit); for high-throughput FP16→int32 conversions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2qq.128
description: Convert packed float16 to signed int64 with truncation and masking (128-bit); used when converting FP16 data to 64-bit integers.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2qq.256
description: Convert packed float16 to signed int64 with truncation and masking (256-bit); vectorized FP16→int64 conversion.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2qq.512
description: Convert packed float16 to signed int64 with truncation and masking (512-bit); high-throughput FP16→int64 conversions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2udq.128
description: Convert packed float16 to unsigned int32 with truncation and masking (128-bit); used for FP16→uint32 conversion in parsing/quantization.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2udq.256
description: Convert packed float16 to unsigned int32 with truncation and masking (256-bit); vectorized FP16→uint32 conversion.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2udq.512
description: Convert packed float16 to unsigned int32 with truncation and masking (512-bit); high-throughput FP16→uint32 conversions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2uqq.128
description: Convert packed float16 to unsigned int64 with truncation and masking (128-bit); used when interpreting FP16 data as uint64.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2uqq.256
description: Convert packed float16 to unsigned int64 with truncation and masking (256-bit); vectorized FP16→uint64 conversion.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2uqq.512
description: Convert FP16 to unsigned 64-bit integers with truncation (round-toward-zero) under a writemask; useful for integerizing half-precision results for indexing, packing, or quantization.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2uw.128
description: Convert FP16 to unsigned 16-bit integers with truncation under a writemask (128-bit); common in quantization, image/ML pipelines, and fast clamping to u16.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2uw.256
description: Convert FP16 to unsigned 16-bit integers with truncation under a writemask (256-bit); used for half→u16 conversion in SIMD quantization paths.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2uw.512
description: Convert FP16 to unsigned 16-bit integers with truncation under a writemask (512-bit); efficient for narrowing half data to u16 buffers.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2w.128
description: Convert FP16 to signed 16-bit integers with truncation under a writemask (128-bit); useful for DSP pipelines and fixed-point conversion.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2w.256
description: Convert FP16 to signed 16-bit integers with truncation under a writemask (256-bit); used in audio/DSP and mixed-precision integerization.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvttph2w.512
description: Convert FP16 to signed 16-bit integers with truncation under a writemask (512-bit); for packing half-precision into i16 with SIMD.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtudq2ph.128
description: Convert unsigned 32-bit integers to FP16 (rounded per FP mode) under a writemask (128-bit); used to compress u32 data to half for storage or mixed-precision compute.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtuqq2ph.128
description: Convert unsigned 64-bit integers to FP16 (rounded per FP mode) under a writemask (128-bit); niche use for downconverting large integers to half when ranges permit.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vcvtuqq2ph.256
description: Convert unsigned 64-bit integers to FP16 (rounded per FP mode) under a writemask (256-bit); used for downconversion/serialization to FP16.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmadd.cph.128
description: Fused complex multiply-add of interleaved FP16 pairs (complex) under a writemask (128-bit); accelerates complex MACs in FFTs, SDR, and communications.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmadd.cph.256
description: Fused complex multiply-add of interleaved FP16 pairs (complex) under a writemask (256-bit); for high-throughput complex arithmetic (FFTs, filters).

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmadd.cph.512
description: Fused complex multiply-add of interleaved FP16 pairs (complex) under a writemask (512-bit); ideal for complex GEMM/FFT inner loops.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmadd.csh
description: Scalar fused complex multiply-add on FP16 complex values (two halves) under a writemask; used in scalar complex DSP kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmul.cph.128
description: Complex multiply of interleaved FP16 pairs under a writemask (128-bit); used in complex multiplication for FFTs, modulation, and correlation.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmul.cph.256
description: Complex multiply of interleaved FP16 pairs under a writemask (256-bit); for SIMD complex arithmetic in DSP/communications.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmul.cph.512
description: Complex multiply of interleaved FP16 pairs under a writemask (512-bit); accelerates large-vector complex ops (FFT, filtering).

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfcmul.csh
description: Scalar complex multiply on FP16 complex values (two halves) under a writemask; used in scalar complex math in DSP.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmadd.cph.128
description: Fused multiply-add on FP16 pairs (operates on adjacent half pairs) under a writemask (128-bit); useful for packed half FMAs in ML/DSP loops.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmadd.cph.256
description: Fused multiply-add on FP16 pairs (operates on adjacent half pairs) under a writemask (256-bit); speeds up elementwise MACs and dot products in half precision.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmadd.cph.512
description: Masked fused complex multiply-add on packed half-precision (512-bit, interleaved real/imag): computes a*b + c per complex lane; used in complex GEMM/FFT; masked-off lanes pass through.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmadd.csh
description: Masked fused complex multiply-add on scalar half-precision complex (interleaved real/imag); useful for scalar complex accumulations; masked-off leaves dest unchanged.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmul.cph.128
description: Masked complex multiply on packed half-precision (128-bit) vectors; used in complex DSP/FFT kernels; masked-off lanes pass through.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmul.cph.256
description: Masked complex multiply on packed half-precision (256-bit) vectors; for vectorized complex math; masked-off lanes pass through.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmul.cph.512
description: Masked complex multiply on packed half-precision (512-bit) vectors; for high-throughput complex arithmetic; masked-off lanes pass through.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mask.vfmul.csh
description: Masked complex multiply on scalar half-precision complex; used in scalar complex ops; masked-off leaves dest unchanged.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfcmadd.cph.128
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add with conjugation on packed half (128-bit): computes a*conj(b) + c; used in correlations/Hermitian ops; masked-off lanes zeroed.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfcmadd.cph.256
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add with conjugation on packed half (256-bit); for complex correlation and Hermitian BLAS; masked-off lanes zeroed.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfcmadd.cph.512
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add with conjugation on packed half (512-bit); for large-width complex accumulations; masked-off lanes zeroed.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfcmadd.csh
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add with conjugation on scalar half complex; used in scalar complex correlation; masked-off yields zero.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfmadd.cph.128
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add on packed half (128-bit): computes a*b + c per complex lane; used in complex dot products; masked-off lanes zeroed.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfmadd.cph.256
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add on packed half (256-bit); for complex GEMM/FFT accumulations; masked-off lanes zeroed.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfmadd.cph.512
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add on packed half (512-bit); for high-throughput complex accumulations; masked-off lanes zeroed.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.maskz.vfmadd.csh
description: Zero-masked fused complex multiply-add on scalar half complex; used in scalar complex accumulations; masked-off yields zero.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.max.ph.128
description: Element-wise max on packed half-precision (128-bit) vectors; used for clamping/activation functions and min/max reductions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.max.ph.256
description: Element-wise max on packed half-precision (256-bit) vectors; used to clamp values or pick larger per lane in SIMD code.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.max.ph.512
description: Element-wise max on packed half-precision (512-bit) vectors; common in activation clipping and vectorized reductions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.min.ph.128
description: Element-wise min on packed half-precision (128-bit) vectors; used for clamping lower bounds and min reductions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.min.ph.256
description: Element-wise min on packed half-precision (256-bit) vectors; pick smaller per lane in SIMD paths.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.min.ph.512
description: Element-wise min on packed half-precision (512-bit) vectors; useful for saturation, clipping, and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.mul.ph.512
description: Element-wise multiply of 32 fp16 lanes in a 512-bit vector; used in ML kernels, DSP, and vector math.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.sqrt.ph.512
description: Element-wise square root for 32 fp16 lanes in a 512-bit vector; used in normalization, RMS, graphics.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.sub.ph.512
description: Element-wise subtract of 32 fp16 lanes in a 512-bit vector; used for differences, residuals, error computation.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcomi.sh
description: Scalar fp16 compare that sets EFLAGS (ordered/gt/lt/eq); used for branching without masks.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtsh2si32
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 32-bit signed int using current MXCSR rounding; used when unpacking fp16 to int.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtsh2si64
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 64-bit signed int using current MXCSR rounding; used for scalar fp16→i64 casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtsh2usi32
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 32-bit unsigned int using current MXCSR rounding; used for fp16→u32 casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtsh2usi64
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 64-bit unsigned int using current MXCSR rounding; used for fp16→u64 casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtsi2sh
description: Convert 32-bit signed int to scalar fp16 (rounded by MXCSR); used to pack ints into half.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtsi642sh
description: Convert 64-bit signed int to scalar fp16 (rounded by MXCSR); used for i64→fp16 conversions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvttsh2si32
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 32-bit signed int with truncation toward zero; used for fast, C-style casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvttsh2si64
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 64-bit signed int with truncation toward zero; used for precise truncating casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvttsh2usi32
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 32-bit unsigned int with truncation toward zero; used for fp16→u32 trunc casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvttsh2usi64
description: Convert scalar fp16 to 64-bit unsigned int with truncation toward zero; used for fp16→u64 trunc casts.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtusi2sh
description: Convert 32-bit unsigned int to scalar fp16 (rounded by MXCSR); used to pack u32 into half.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vcvtusi642sh
description: Convert 64-bit unsigned int to scalar fp16 (rounded by MXCSR); used for u64→fp16 conversions.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vfmadd.f16
description: Scalar fp16 fused multiply-add a*b + c with single rounding; used in dot products and scalar kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vfmadd.ph.512
description: Fused multiply-add across 32 fp16 lanes in 512-bit vectors; used in GEMM/conv inner loops and vector accumulations.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vfmaddsub.ph.128
description: Alternating fused add/sub across 8 fp16 lanes (128-bit); used in complex arithmetic and alternating-sum kernels.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vfmaddsub.ph.256
description: Alternating fused add/sub across 16 fp16 lanes (256-bit); used in complex math and mixed add/sub vector ops.

name: llvm.x86.avx512fp16.vfmaddsub.ph.512
description: Vector fused multiply-add-subtract on 512-bit FP16 lanes (a*b+c on even lanes, a*b−c on odd); useful for complex arithmetic, DSP, and certain linear algebra kernels.

name: llvm.x86.axor32
description: 32-bit XOR that suppresses flag updates (APX); useful to avoid clobbering EFLAGS (e.g., zeroing/XOR in code that relies on condition codes).

name: llvm.x86.axor64
description: 64-bit XOR that suppresses flag updates (APX); useful when doing bit ops or zeroing without touching flags near branches/CMOVs.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.bextr.32
description: Bit field extract (variable start/length) into low bits; useful for fast bitfield decode and index extraction.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.bextr.64
description: 64-bit bit field extract into low bits; useful for parsing packed headers, bit slicing, and fast shifts+masks.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.bzhi.32
description: Zero high bits above a given index (x & ((1<<idx)-1)); useful for masking to a variable bit-width and bounded arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.bzhi.64
description: 64-bit zero-high-bits by index; useful for variable-width masking and truncating values without branches.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.pdep.32
description: Parallel bit deposit: scatter source bits into positions selected by mask; useful for Morton codes, bitboard mapping, and index expansion.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.pdep.64
description: 64-bit parallel bit deposit; useful in bit packing, swizzles, and building sparse-bit layouts.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.pext.32
description: Parallel bit extract: gather masked bits, packed to low positions; useful for compression, lookup indexing, and rank/select structures.

name: llvm.x86.bmi.pext.64
description: 64-bit parallel bit extract; useful for fast bit compaction, hashing, and deinterleaving.

name: llvm.x86.cast.tile.to.vector
description: Reinterpret an AMX tile as a vector value; useful for spilling, memcopies, or interoperating AMX tiles with vector code/ABI.

name: llvm.x86.cast.vector.to.tile
description: Reinterpret a vector value as an AMX tile; useful for restoring tiles from memory or feeding AMX compute from vector data.

name: llvm.x86.cldemote
description: Cache line demote hint (push line toward L2); useful to reduce L1 pollution for data no longer hot soon.

name: llvm.x86.clflushopt
description: Optimized cache line flush (write back + invalidate with relaxed ordering); useful for persistence barriers and cache management with lower latency than CLFLUSH.

name: llvm.x86.clrssbsy
description: Clear shadow stack busy bit (CET); useful for managing thread/context shadow stacks and making frames reusable.

name: llvm.x86.clui
description: Clear user-interrupt enable (UINTR); useful to temporarily block user-mode interrupts around critical sections.

name: llvm.x86.clwb
description: Cache line write-back without invalidation; useful for persisting data to NVRAM/PMEM while keeping it cached.

name: llvm.x86.clzero
description: Zero an entire cache line without reading memory (AMD); useful for fast secure memset/zeroing with minimal bandwidth.

name: llvm.x86.cmpccxadd32
description: Atomic compare-conditional add: if a comparison condition holds, add to memory in one RMW op; useful for lock-free counters, bounded atomics, and contention-friendly updates.

name: llvm.x86.cmpccxadd64
description: Conditionally performs an add-with-exchange on a 64-bit destination based on a chosen condition code; useful for branchless conditional updates and lock-free counters.

name: llvm.x86.directstore32
description: MOVDIRI 32-bit direct store that bypasses caches and writes directly to memory; used for MMIO/device doorbells and persistent memory logging.

name: llvm.x86.directstore64
description: MOVDIRI 64-bit direct store that bypasses caches and writes directly to memory; used for MMIO/device doorbells and persistent memory logging.

name: llvm.x86.encodekey128
description: Encodes a 128-bit AES key using Intel Key Locker into a wrapped/handle form; used to prepare keys for hardware-accelerated AES without exposing raw keys.

name: llvm.x86.encodekey256
description: Encodes a 256-bit AES key using Intel Key Locker into a wrapped/handle form; used to prepare keys for hardware-accelerated AES without exposing raw keys.

name: llvm.x86.enqcmd
description: ENQCMD: enqueue a 64B command descriptor to a device submission portal from user mode; used for userspace submission to accelerators (e.g., DSA/IDX).

name: llvm.x86.enqcmds
description: ENQCMDS: supervisor-only enqueue of a 64B command descriptor; used by kernels/drivers to submit work to devices.

name: llvm.x86.flags.read.u32
description: Reads EFLAGS into a 32-bit value; useful to save/inspect flags around inline asm or to test carry/overflow/parity without branches.

name: llvm.x86.flags.read.u64
description: Reads RFLAGS into a 64-bit value; useful to save/inspect flags in 64-bit code around inline asm or synthesized condition handling.

name: llvm.x86.flags.write.u32
description: Writes EFLAGS from a 32-bit value; used to restore saved flags or synthesize condition codes for low-level bit/crypto routines.

name: llvm.x86.flags.write.u64
description: Writes RFLAGS from a 64-bit value; used to restore saved flags or synthesize condition codes in 64-bit code.

name: llvm.x86.fma.vfmaddsub.pd
description: VFMADDSUBPD on packed doubles: multiply then alternately add/sub per lane; efficient for complex arithmetic and DSP kernels.

name: llvm.x86.fma.vfmaddsub.pd.256
description: 256-bit VFMADDSUBPD on packed doubles; efficient for complex arithmetic and DSP kernels.

name: llvm.x86.fma.vfmaddsub.ps
description: VFMADDSUBPS on packed singles: multiply then alternately add/sub per lane; efficient for complex arithmetic and DSP kernels.

name: llvm.x86.fma.vfmaddsub.ps.256
description: 256-bit VFMADDSUBPS on packed singles; efficient for complex arithmetic and DSP kernels.

name: llvm.x86.fxrstor
description: FXRSTOR: restore x87/SSE (FPU, XMM, MXCSR) state from memory; used by OSes for context switches and signal/exception handling.

name: llvm.x86.fxrstor64
description: FXRSTOR64: 64-bit mode restore of x87/SSE state (64-bit pointers in image); used for context switches in 64-bit kernels.

name: llvm.x86.fxsave
description: FXSAVE: save x87/SSE (FPU, XMM, MXCSR) state to memory; used by OSes for context switches, checkpointing, and green threads.

name: llvm.x86.fxsave64
description: FXSAVE64: 64-bit mode save of x87/SSE state (64-bit pointers in image); used for context switches in 64-bit kernels.

name: llvm.x86.incsspd
description: INCSSP (dword count): increment the CET shadow stack pointer by a 32-bit count; used to unwind/adjust shadow stacks during exceptions, longjmp, or tail calls.

name: llvm.x86.incsspq
description: Increment shadow stack pointer (CET) by an immediate; used to skip return addresses during unwinding, error recovery, or special prolog/epilog handling.

name: llvm.x86.int
description: Software interrupt (INT imm8); used by kernels/firmware to enter interrupt handlers or for debug/traps (rare in user space).

name: llvm.x86.invpcid
description: Invalidate TLB entries with PCID granularity; used by kernels/hypervisors to precisely flush translations after page/CR3 changes.

name: llvm.x86.ldtilecfg
description: Load AMX tile configuration from memory into the tilecfg state; used to set tile shapes/strides before AMX matrix ops.

name: llvm.x86.ldtilecfg.internal
description: Compiler-internal form of ldtilecfg (same effect, different constraints); used by codegen, not meant for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.llwpcb
description: Load Lightweight Profiling (LWP) control block pointer; used to initialize AMD LWP profiling context.

name: llvm.x86.loadiwkey
description: Load the internal wrapping key for Intel Key Locker; used by OS/boot loaders to enable protected AES key handles.

name: llvm.x86.lwpins32
description: Insert 32-bit value into an AMD LWP control/event register; used by profilers to configure lightweight sampling.

name: llvm.x86.lwpins64
description: Insert 64-bit value into an AMD LWP control/event register; used by profilers to configure lightweight sampling.

name: llvm.x86.lwpval32
description: Read/return a 32-bit value from an AMD LWP register; used to query profiling state/counters.

name: llvm.x86.lwpval64
description: Read/return a 64-bit value from an AMD LWP register; used to query profiling state/counters.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.emms
description: Empty MMX state and restore x87 tag word; must be executed after MMX before using x87 FP.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.maskmovq
description: Masked byte store from MMX to memory (selected by high bits of mask); used for alpha blending and conditional stores with minimal cache pollution.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.movnt.dq
description: Non-temporal (streaming) store of an MMX quadword to memory; used for write-combining in large streaming writes.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.packssdw
description: Pack 32-bit signed ints to 16-bit with signed saturation; used for narrowing in image/audio processing.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.packsswb
description: Pack 16-bit signed ints to 8-bit with signed saturation; used for narrowing pixel/audio data.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.packuswb
description: Pack 16-bit signed ints to 8-bit with unsigned saturation; used for clamping to 8-bit pixel ranges.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.padd.b
description: Add packed 8-bit integers with wrap-around; used in pixel ops, mixing, and checksums.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.padd.d
description: Add packed 32-bit integers with wrap-around; used in vector integer arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.padd.q
description: Add packed 64-bit integers with wrap-around; used in wide-precision integer accumulations.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.padd.w
description: Packed 16-bit integer add with wraparound; vector integer arithmetic in image/audio kernels.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.padds.b
description: Packed signed 8-bit add with saturation; avoids overflow in pixel/audio accumulation and DSP.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.padds.w
description: Packed signed 16-bit add with saturation; fixed-point DSP, image/audio filters.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.paddus.b
description: Packed unsigned 8-bit add with saturation; pixel ops, blending, color accumulation.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.paddus.w
description: Packed unsigned 16-bit add with saturation; image processing and fixed-point filters.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.palignr.b
description: Byte-align right across two MMX regs (concatenate then shift by N bytes); data realignment, sliding windows, crypto shuffles.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pand
description: Packed bitwise AND; masking lanes, clearing bits.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pandn
description: Packed bitwise ANDNOT (~a & b); inverted-mask operations and masked merges.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pavg.b
description: Packed unsigned byte average with rounding; image blur/motion compensation.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pavg.w
description: Packed unsigned 16-bit average with rounding; smoothing/filters on pixel or sensor data.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pcmpeq.b
description: Packed byte equality compare -> all-ones/zero mask; building masks, byte/string scans.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pcmpeq.d
description: Packed 32-bit equality compare -> mask; filtering/select operations.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pcmpeq.w
description: Packed 16-bit equality compare -> mask; per-element equality and masking.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pcmpgt.b
description: Packed signed byte greater-than -> mask; thresholding and compare-based selection.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pcmpgt.d
description: Packed signed 32-bit greater-than -> mask; vector comparisons and selects.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pcmpgt.w
description: Packed signed 16-bit greater-than -> mask; image thresholding and DSP compares.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pextr.w
description: Extract a 16-bit element from an MMX reg to an integer (by index); scalar access/reductions.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pinsr.w
description: Insert a 16-bit integer into an MMX lane (by index); building/updating vectors.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmadd.wd
description: Multiply adjacent signed 16-bit pairs and add to 32-bit results; dot products, FIR filters, IDCT.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmaxs.w
description: Packed signed 16-bit per-lane max; clamping and range limiting in DSP/image code.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmaxu.b
description: Pairwise max of unsigned 8-bit elements; useful for clamping, image processing, and max filters.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmins.w
description: Pairwise min of signed 16-bit elements; useful for saturation/clipping in audio and vector min operations.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pminu.b
description: Pairwise min of unsigned 8-bit elements; useful for clamping, thresholding, and min filters in images.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmovmskb
description: Extracts MSBs of 8 bytes into an integer mask; useful for packing compare results, scans, and branch decisions.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmulh.w
description: High 16 bits of signed 16×16 multiplies; useful for Q15/Q1.15 fixed-point math without overflow.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmulhu.w
description: High 16 bits of unsigned 16×16 multiplies; useful for fixed-point scaling and normalization.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmull.w
description: Low 16 bits of 16×16 multiplies; useful for 16-bit arithmetic, modular math, and pixel ops.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pmulu.dq
description: Unsigned 32×32→64 widening multiply (low lane); useful for 32-bit products, hashing, and big-integer steps.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.por
description: Bitwise OR of MMX vectors; useful for combining masks, flags, and blending conditions.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psad.bw
description: Sum of absolute differences of packed bytes; useful for motion estimation and block matching in codecs/vision.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psll.d
description: Logical left shift of 32-bit lanes by variable count; useful for scaling, mask creation, and bit packing.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psll.q
description: Logical left shift of 64-bit lane by variable count; useful for 64-bit alignment and bitfield placement.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psll.w
description: Logical left shift of 16-bit lanes by variable count; useful for fixed-point scaling and mask generation.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pslli.d
description: Logical left shift of 32-bit lanes by immediate; useful for compile-time scaling and mask creation.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pslli.q
description: Logical left shift of 64-bit lane by immediate; useful for fixed shifts in 64-bit data and bitfields.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pslli.w
description: Logical left shift of 16-bit lanes by immediate; useful for fixed-point scaling with known counts.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psra.d
description: Arithmetic right shift of 32-bit lanes by variable count; useful for signed divides and fixed-point normalization.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psra.w
description: Arithmetic right shift of 16-bit lanes by variable count; useful for signed scaling and rounding down.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrai.d
description: Arithmetic right shift of 32-bit lanes by immediate; useful for predictable signed scaling/division.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrai.w
description: Arithmetic right shift of 16-bit lanes by immediate; useful for fixed-point normalization with known count.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrl.d
description: Packed logical right shift of 2x32-bit lanes by a variable count; used for unsigned divide-by-powers-of-two, scaling, and bit-field extraction.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrl.q
description: Logical right shift of the 64-bit MMX value by a variable count; used for aligning 64-bit data and bitpacking.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrl.w
description: Packed logical right shift of 4x16-bit lanes by a variable count; used for pixel/intensity downscaling and averaging.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrli.d
description: Packed logical right shift of 2x32-bit lanes by an immediate count; efficient scaling and mask/extract operations.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrli.q
description: Logical right shift of the 64-bit MMX value by an immediate count; 64-bit alignment and packing.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psrli.w
description: Packed logical right shift of 4x16-bit lanes by an immediate count; unsigned divide-by-two steps in image/audio pipelines.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psub.b
description: Packed subtract of 8x8-bit integers (modulo wraparound); byte-wise differences for filters and gradients.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psub.d
description: Packed subtract of 2x32-bit integers (wraparound); vector diffs for 32-bit data and accumulators.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psub.q
description: Subtract of 64-bit integers (wraparound); 64-bit counters and wide-precision diffs.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psub.w
description: Packed subtract of 4x16-bit integers (wraparound); sample/pixel gradients and DSP inner loops.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psubs.b
description: Packed signed saturating subtract of bytes; clamps to [-128,127]; used in DSP with signed 8-bit data.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psubs.w
description: Packed signed saturating subtract of 16-bit; clamps to [-32768,32767]; used in audio/DSP to avoid overflow.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psubus.b
description: Packed unsigned saturating subtract of bytes; clamps at 0; common in image ops (e.g., abs-diff tricks, edge detection).

name: llvm.x86.mmx.psubus.w
description: Packed unsigned saturating subtract of 16-bit; clamps at 0; used with 16-bit pixels and fixed-point pipelines.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.punpckhbw
description: Unpack/interleave high 4 bytes from two MMX regs into 4 words; channel deinterleave and widening 8-bit to 16-bit.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.punpckhdq
description: Interleave high 32-bit elements from two MMX regs into one 64-bit result; building 64-bit pairs/swizzles.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.punpckhwd
description: Unpack/interleave high 16-bit elements from two regs into two 32-bit doublewords; preparing for 32-bit arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.punpcklbw
description: Unpack/interleave low 4 bytes from two regs into 4 words; widen 8-bit pixels to 16-bit (with zero), channel interleave.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.punpckldq
description: Interleave low 32-bit elements from two regs into one 64-bit result; packing two 32-bit values, swizzling.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.punpcklwd
description: Unpack/interleave low 16-bit elements from two regs into two 32-bit doublewords; lane rearrangement for 32-bit ops.

name: llvm.x86.mmx.pxor
description: Bitwise XOR of two 64-bit MMX vectors; used in legacy SIMD code for masking, checksums, image/video ops.

name: llvm.x86.monitorx
description: Set up a hardware monitor on an address (MONITORX) for MWAITX; used for efficient spin-wait/sleep in locks and runtimes.

name: llvm.x86.movdir64b
description: Copy 64 bytes from src to dst using direct, write-combining stores; used for fast copies to device/persistent memory buffers and log writes.

name: llvm.x86.mwaitx
description: Wait on a monitored address with timeout/control (MWAITX); used to implement low-power waits/backoff in spinlocks and user-level schedulers.

name: llvm.x86.pclmulqdq
description: Carry-less 64×64-bit multiply in a 128-bit vector (select halves); used for CRC, GHASH (AES-GCM), and GF(2) arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.pclmulqdq.256
description: AVX lane-wise carry-less 64×64-bit multiplies on 256-bit vectors; accelerates CRC/GHASH over wider data.

name: llvm.x86.pclmulqdq.512
description: AVX-512 lane-wise carry-less 64×64-bit multiplies on 512-bit vectors; high-throughput CRC/GHASH.

name: llvm.x86.ptwrite32
description: Emit a 32-bit value into Intel Processor Trace via PTWRITE; used for user-level tracing/annotations and virtualization.

name: llvm.x86.ptwrite64
description: Emit a 64-bit value into Intel Processor Trace via PTWRITE; used for user-level tracing/annotations and profiling.

name: llvm.x86.rdfsbase.32
description: Read the FS segment base (32-bit form); used to obtain TLS base quickly in runtimes.

name: llvm.x86.rdfsbase.64
description: Read the FS segment base (64-bit form); used for fast TLS access and thread metadata.

name: llvm.x86.rdgsbase.32
description: Read the GS segment base (32-bit form); used for TLS/GS-based per-CPU data.

name: llvm.x86.rdgsbase.64
description: Read the GS segment base (64-bit form); used for TLS and per-CPU data structures.

name: llvm.x86.rdpid
description: Read processor ID/TSC_AUX (RDPID), non-serializing; used to get per-core/thread IDs for per-CPU data and scheduling.

name: llvm.x86.rdpkru
description: Read PKRU (Protection Keys for Userspace) register; used to query memory key permissions in MPK-aware runtimes.

name: llvm.x86.rdpmc
description: Read a performance-monitoring counter; used for low-overhead perf measurements and profiling.

name: llvm.x86.rdpru
description: AMD RDPRU: read selected system registers in user mode (e.g., TSC, topology info); used for timing/topology without syscalls.

name: llvm.x86.rdrand.16
description: Hardware-generated 16-bit random value; used for seeding PRNGs and crypto where RDRAND is acceptable.

name: llvm.x86.rdrand.32
description: Hardware-generated 32-bit random value; used for seeding PRNGs and crypto where RDRAND is acceptable.

name: llvm.x86.rdrand.64
description: Hardware-generated 64-bit random value; used for seeding PRNGs and crypto where RDRAND is acceptable.

name: llvm.x86.rdseed.16
description: Returns a 16-bit hardware-generated random seed (RDSEED); use to seed PRNGs/DRBGs and cryptographic protocols.

name: llvm.x86.rdseed.32
description: Returns a 32-bit hardware-generated random seed (RDSEED); use to seed PRNGs/DRBGs and cryptographic protocols.

name: llvm.x86.rdseed.64
description: Returns a 64-bit hardware-generated random seed (RDSEED); use to seed PRNGs/DRBGs and cryptographic protocols.

name: llvm.x86.rdsspd
description: Reads the 32-bit shadow stack pointer (RDSSP dword); used with Intel CET shadow stacks for diagnostics or stack switching.

name: llvm.x86.rdsspq
description: Reads the 64-bit shadow stack pointer (RDSSP qword); used with Intel CET shadow stacks for diagnostics or stack switching.

name: llvm.x86.rdtsc
description: Reads the time-stamp counter (RDTSC); high-resolution timing/profiling (not serializing—pair with fences if ordering is needed).

name: llvm.x86.rdtscp
description: Reads TSC and TSC_AUX with serialization (RDTSCP); precise timing and CPU/core identification with ordering guarantees.

name: llvm.x86.rstorssp
description: Restores the saved shadow stack pointer from memory (RSTORSSP); CET context restore and shadow-stack switching.

name: llvm.x86.saveprevssp
description: Saves the previous shadow stack pointer (SAVEPREVSSP); used when switching to a new CET shadow stack.

name: llvm.x86.seh.ehguard
description: Emits/marks Windows EH Guard metadata for protected exception continuations; used by the compiler for Control Flow Guard–EH.

name: llvm.x86.seh.ehregnode
description: Provides the Windows SEH registration node address on x86; used internally to build SEH chain frames.

name: llvm.x86.seh.lsda
description: Gets the language-specific data area (LSDA) for exception handling; used by EH personalities/funclets (Windows targets).

name: llvm.x86.senduipi
description: Sends a user-mode IPI (SENDUIPI) to a target CPU (UINTR); low-latency cross-thread/CPU notifications from user space.

name: llvm.x86.serialize
description: Fully serializes instruction execution (SERIALIZE); strict ordering for self-modifying code, timing, and mitigation barriers.

name: llvm.x86.setssbsy
description: Sets the shadow stack busy bit (SETSSBSY); marks a CET shadow stack as in use before switching to it.

name: llvm.x86.sha1msg1
description: First part of SHA-1 message schedule expansion; accelerates SHA-1 block processing.

name: llvm.x86.sha1msg2
description: Second part of SHA-1 message schedule expansion; accelerates SHA-1 block processing.

name: llvm.x86.sha1nexte
description: Computes the next E state value in SHA-1; used within the SHA-1 compression loop.

name: llvm.x86.sha1rnds4
description: Performs 4 rounds of SHA-1 compression; speeds up the core round function.

name: llvm.x86.sha256msg1
description: First part of SHA-256 message schedule expansion; accelerates SHA-256 block processing.

name: llvm.x86.sha256msg2
description: Computes the second part of the SHA-256 message schedule for 4 dwords; used to accelerate SHA-256 block processing.

name: llvm.x86.sha256rnds2
description: Performs two SHA-256 compression rounds on the state using the message schedule; used for hardware-accelerated SHA-256.

name: llvm.x86.slwpcb
description: Stores the AMD LWP (Lightweight Profiling) control block to memory; used to save LWP profiling state/context.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cmp.ps
description: Compares packed single-precision floats by an immediate predicate, returning a lane-wise mask; used for SIMD comparisons/masking.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cmp.ss
description: Compares the lowest scalar floats by a predicate and merges the rest; used for scalar SSE compares with specific NaN semantics.

name: llvm.x86.sse.comieq.ss
description: Ordered, signaling scalar float equal compare returning 0/1; used for fast eq checks (raises on NaN).

name: llvm.x86.sse.comige.ss
description: Ordered, signaling scalar float greater-or-equal compare returning 0/1; used for scalar branching (raises on NaN).

name: llvm.x86.sse.comigt.ss
description: Ordered, signaling scalar float greater-than compare returning 0/1; used for scalar branching (raises on NaN).

name: llvm.x86.sse.comile.ss
description: Ordered, signaling scalar float less-or-equal compare returning 0/1; used for scalar branching (raises on NaN).

name: llvm.x86.sse.comilt.ss
description: Ordered, signaling scalar float less-than compare returning 0/1; used for scalar branching (raises on NaN).

name: llvm.x86.sse.comineq.ss
description: Ordered, signaling scalar float not-equal compare returning 0/1; used for scalar branching (raises on NaN).

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvtpd2pi
description: Converts 2 packed doubles to 2 32-bit ints in MMX using current rounding; used for legacy SIMD double→int conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvtpi2pd
description: Converts 2 32-bit ints in MMX to 2 packed doubles; used for legacy SIMD int→double conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvtpi2ps
description: Converts 2 32-bit ints in MMX to 2 packed floats; used for legacy SIMD int→float conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvtps2pi
description: Converts 2 packed floats to 2 32-bit ints in MMX using current rounding; used for legacy SIMD float→int conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvtss2si
description: Converts scalar float to 32-bit int using current rounding; used for scalar float→int casts.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvtss2si64
description: Converts scalar float to 64-bit int using current rounding; used for scalar float→int64 casts.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvttpd2pi
description: Converts 2 packed doubles to 2 32-bit ints in MMX using truncation (toward zero); used for C-style casts.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvttps2pi
description: Converts 2 packed floats to 2 32-bit ints in MMX using truncation (toward zero); used for SIMD casts with TZ semantics.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvttss2si
description: Converts scalar float to 32-bit int using truncation (toward zero); used for scalar casts with TZ semantics.

name: llvm.x86.sse.cvttss2si64
description: Truncates a scalar float to a signed 64-bit int (round toward zero); useful for fast float→int casts (e.g., indices) with defined truncation.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ldmxcsr
description: Loads the MXCSR control/status register from memory; used to set FP rounding mode, exception masks, FTZ/DAZ before SSE math.

name: llvm.x86.sse.max.ps
description: Per-lane max of two float vectors; used for clamps, vectorized max, and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.sse.max.ss
description: Scalar max of low float; upper lanes pass through left operand; used for scalar max in XMM while preserving other lanes (e.g., reductions).

name: llvm.x86.sse.min.ps
description: Per-lane min of two float vectors; used for clamps, vectorized min, and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.sse.min.ss
description: Scalar min of low float; upper lanes pass through left operand; used for scalar min in XMM while preserving other lanes.

name: llvm.x86.sse.movmsk.ps
description: Extracts sign bits of 4 floats into a 4-bit mask; used for any/all tests, branch decisions, and packing compare results.

name: llvm.x86.sse.pshuf.w
description: Shuffles 4×16-bit lanes within a 64-bit MMX value per immediate; used for swizzles in media/pixel processing and endian/format rearranges.

name: llvm.x86.sse.rcp.ps
description: Fast approximate reciprocal (1/x) for 4 floats; used for vectorized division with optional Newton–Raphson refinement.

name: llvm.x86.sse.rcp.ss
description: Fast approximate reciprocal of scalar float (low lane); used when a quick 1/x is needed, optionally refined.

name: llvm.x86.sse.rsqrt.ps
description: Fast approximate reciprocal square root for 4 floats; used in normalization, lighting/physics, and length estimates with refinement.

name: llvm.x86.sse.rsqrt.ss
description: Fast approximate reciprocal square root of scalar float (low lane); used for quick 1/sqrt(x) with optional refinement.

name: llvm.x86.sse.sfence
description: Store fence; orders prior stores before later stores (esp. non-temporal/PMEM); used for streaming writes and persistence ordering.

name: llvm.x86.sse.stmxcsr
description: Stores the MXCSR control/status register to memory; used to snapshot FP flags/mode or to save/restore the FP environment.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ucomieq.ss
description: Scalar float compare “unordered or equal”; returns 1 if a==b or either is NaN; used for NaN-aware equality tests.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ucomige.ss
description: Scalar float compare “unordered or ≥”; returns 1 if a≥b or either is NaN; used for NaN-aware threshold checks.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ucomigt.ss
description: Scalar float compare “unordered or >”; returns 1 if a>b or either is NaN; used for NaN-aware strict greater-than tests.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ucomile.ss
description: Scalar float compare “unordered or ≤”; returns 1 if a≤b or either is NaN; used for NaN-aware upper-bound checks.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ucomilt.ss
description: Scalar float compare “unordered or <”; returns 1 if a<b or either is NaN; used for NaN-aware strict less-than tests.

name: llvm.x86.sse.ucomineq.ss
description: Scalar float compare “unordered or !=”; returns 1 if a!=b or either is NaN; used for NaN-aware inequality tests.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.clflush
description: Flushes a cache line containing the given address (write-back + invalidate); used for streaming writes, DMA/persistent memory, and cache-coherency control.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cmp.pd
description: Packed compare of two <2 x double> with an immediate predicate; returns a mask vector, used for vectorized conditionals/blends.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cmp.sd
description: Scalar compare of low-lane doubles with a predicate; produces a mask in the low lane, used for scalar FP conditionals.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.comieq.sd
description: Ordered scalar FP compare equal (double) -> i32 0/1; NaNs make it false; used for branching without extracting masks.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.comige.sd
description: Ordered scalar FP compare >= (double) -> i32 0/1; NaNs make it false; used for scalar predicates.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.comigt.sd
description: Ordered scalar FP compare > (double) -> i32 0/1; NaNs make it false; used for scalar predicates.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.comile.sd
description: Ordered scalar FP compare <= (double) -> i32 0/1; NaNs make it false; used for scalar predicates.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.comilt.sd
description: Ordered scalar FP compare < (double) -> i32 0/1; NaNs make it false; used for scalar predicates.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.comineq.sd
description: Ordered scalar FP compare != (double) -> i32 0/1; NaNs make it false; used when “unordered !=” is not desired.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvtpd2dq
description: Convert packed doubles to signed 32-bit ints using current FP rounding; used for vectorized float->int conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvtpd2ps
description: Convert packed doubles to packed floats (downcast with rounding); used to reduce precision or interop with float code.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvtps2dq
description: Convert packed floats to signed 32-bit ints using current FP rounding; used for vectorized indexing/quantization.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvtsd2si
description: Convert scalar double to 32-bit int using current FP rounding; used for scalar float->int conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvtsd2si64
description: Convert scalar double to 64-bit int using current FP rounding; used for scalar float->int64 conversion.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvtsd2ss
description: Convert scalar double to float (low lane), copying upper lanes; used for downcasting a single value.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvttpd2dq
description: Convert packed doubles to signed 32-bit ints with truncation toward zero; used for index/round-toward-zero needs.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvttps2dq
description: Convert packed floats to signed 32-bit ints with truncation toward zero; used for fast, truncating conversions.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvttsd2si
description: Convert scalar double to 32-bit int with truncation toward zero; used where exact truncation is required.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.cvttsd2si64
description: Convert scalar double to 64-bit int with truncation toward zero; used for truncating float->int64.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.lfence
description: Serializing load fence and speculation barrier; used to order loads, precise timing, and mitigate speculation (Spectre) effects.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.maskmov.dqu
description: Conditionally store selected bytes from an XMM to memory where the corresponding mask byte has its MSB set; used for masked/tail stores in memcopy/string ops without read-modify.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.max.pd
description: Pairwise maximum of packed double-precision elements; useful for vectorized clamping and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.max.sd
description: Scalar maximum of the low double element (upper lanes pass-through); used for scalar math and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.mfence
description: Full memory fence ordering loads/stores; used in multithreaded synchronization and after non-temporal stores.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.min.pd
description: Pairwise minimum of packed double-precision elements; useful for vectorized clamping and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.min.sd
description: Scalar minimum of the low double element (upper lanes pass-through); used for scalar math and reductions.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.movmsk.pd
description: Extracts sign bits of two packed doubles into a 2-bit integer mask; used to test compare results (any/all) and control flow.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.packssdw.128
description: Packs two vectors of signed 32-bit ints into signed 16-bit with saturation; used for narrowing in image/audio pipelines.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.packsswb.128
description: Packs two vectors of signed 16-bit ints into signed 8-bit with saturation; used for narrowing, color/channel packing.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.packuswb.128
description: Packs two vectors of 16-bit ints into unsigned 8-bit with saturation; used for pixel conversion and clamping to [0,255].

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pause
description: Spin-wait hint to reduce power and contention; used in busy-wait loops.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pavg.b
description: Averages packed unsigned bytes ((a+b+1)>>1); used in image/video smoothing and motion estimation.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pavg.w
description: Averages packed unsigned 16-bit words; used in filtering and image/video processing.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pmadd.wd
description: Multiplies adjacent signed 16-bit pairs and horizontally adds into 32-bit results; used for dot products, FIR filters, IDCT.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pmovmskb.128
description: Extracts the MSB of each of 16 bytes into a 16-bit mask; used to compress compare results and branch/mask decisions.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pmulh.w
description: High 16 bits of signed 16×16 multiplies per lane; used for fixed-point (Q15) arithmetic and scaling.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pmulhu.w
description: High 16 bits of unsigned 16×16 multiplies per lane; used for fixed-point and normalization.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psad.bw
description: Sum of absolute differences of packed unsigned bytes (per 64-bit half); used for SAD metrics in video/block matching.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psll.d
description: Logical left shift of packed 32-bit integers by a count; used for scaling and bitfield operations.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psll.q
description: Logical left shift of packed 64-bit integers by a count; used for scaling and bitfield operations.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psll.w
