description: Packed 16-bit logical left shift by a variable count (same for all lanes); multiply by 2^n, bit packing/masking in SIMD loops.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pslli.d
description: Packed 32-bit logical left shift by immediate; fast power-of-two scaling, aligning fields in vectors.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pslli.q
description: Packed 64-bit logical left shift by immediate; 64-bit mask/bitfield ops, hashing/crypto prep.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.pslli.w
description: Packed 16-bit logical left shift by immediate; scaling small ints, pixel/ID packing.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psra.d
description: Packed 32-bit arithmetic right shift by a variable count (sign-preserving); signed divide by 2^n, fixed-point rounding.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psra.w
description: Packed 16-bit arithmetic right shift by a variable count; signed scaling and fixed-point arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrai.d
description: Packed 32-bit arithmetic right shift by immediate; fast signed divide/round in SIMD code.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrai.w
description: Packed 16-bit arithmetic right shift by immediate; signed downscaling and rounding.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrl.d
description: Packed 32-bit logical right shift by a variable count; unsigned divide by 2^n, bit-field extraction.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrl.q
description: Packed 64-bit logical right shift by a variable count; 64-bit bit slicing, checksums/crypto primitives.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrl.w
description: Packed 16-bit logical right shift by a variable count; unsigned scaling, mask extraction.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrli.d
description: Packed 32-bit logical right shift by immediate; clear low bits, index/mask operations.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrli.q
description: Packed 64-bit logical right shift by immediate; 64-bit field extraction, hash/mask steps.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.psrli.w
description: Packed 16-bit logical right shift by immediate; extract fields, downscale unsigned values.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.ucomieq.sd
description: Unordered compare equal on lower double; true if a==b or either is NaN; branch/control with NaN-as-true semantics.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.ucomige.sd
description: Unordered compare ≥ on lower double; true if a≥b or NaN present; scalar FP control flow.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.ucomigt.sd
description: Unordered compare > on lower double; true if a>b or NaN present; FP compares where NaN counts as true.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.ucomile.sd
description: Unordered compare ≤ on lower double; true if a≤b or NaN present; ordering with NaN treated as true.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.ucomilt.sd
description: Unordered compare < on lower double; true if a<b or NaN present; scalar FP branching with NaN-as-true.

name: llvm.x86.sse2.ucomineq.sd
description: Unordered compare ≠ on lower double; true if a!=b or NaN present; NaN-aware inequality tests.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.addsub.pd
description: Alternating subtract/add of packed doubles (a0-b0, a1+b1); useful in complex arithmetic and alternating-sign vector ops.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.addsub.ps
description: Alternating subtract/add of packed floats (a0-b0, a1+b1, a2-b2, a3+b3); useful in complex math and alternating-sign kernels.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.hadd.pd
description: Horizontal add of adjacent doubles producing [a0+a1, b0+b1]; useful for pairwise reductions and dot-product accumulation.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.hadd.ps
description: Horizontal add of adjacent floats producing [a0+a1, a2+a3, b0+b1, b2+b3]; useful for reductions and dot-product steps.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.hsub.pd
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent doubles producing [a0-a1, b0-b1]; useful in complex arithmetic and vector differences.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.hsub.ps
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent floats producing [a0-a1, a2-a3, b0-b1, b2-b3]; useful in complex math and transform kernels.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.ldu.dq
description: Load unaligned 128-bit (LDDQU), optimized for cache-line splits; useful for misaligned vector loads in text/image processing.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.monitor
description: MONITOR: set an address-range monitor for a memory location; used with MWAIT for efficient low-power spin/wait loops.

name: llvm.x86.sse3.mwait
description: MWAIT: sleep until a write/interrupt occurs on a monitored address; used in spin-wait primitives and runtimes.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.blendvpd
description: Per-lane blend of packed doubles under a variable mask; useful for predicated selects and masked math.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.blendvps
description: Per-lane blend of packed floats under a variable mask; useful for branchless vectorized conditionals.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.dppd
description: Dot product of double vectors with imm-mask controlling lanes and result placement; useful for 2D dot products/projections.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.dpps
description: Dot product of float vectors with imm-mask (select lanes, broadcast/zero results); common in 3D/4D graphics and linear algebra.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.insertps
description: Insert a selected float lane and optionally zero others via imm; useful for building/scattering scalars and shuffles.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.mpsadbw
description: Multiple SAD of unsigned bytes into 16-bit sums; useful for block matching, motion estimation, and image/video SAD.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.packusdw
description: Pack 32-bit signed ints to 16-bit unsigned with saturation; useful for narrowing accumulators to pixel/fixed-point formats.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.pblendvb
description: Variable byte-wise blend of integer vectors under a mask (high bit selects); useful for masked moves and conditional merges.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.phminposuw
description: Horizontal min and index of unsigned 16-bit lanes (min value and its position); useful for winner selection/SAD minima.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.ptestc
description: Test “all set bits in b are also set in a” ((~a & b)==0); useful to check full coverage/all-lanes-active under a mask.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.ptestnzc
description: Test “mixed bits” (both (a & b)!=0 and (~a & b)!=0); useful to detect partial masks for mixed-active lanes.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.ptestz
description: Tests whether (a & b) == 0 on 128-bit vectors; used to check if a mask has no bits set (e.g., no lanes match) for fast branch decisions.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.round.pd
description: Rounds packed double-precision floats per imm8 mode (nearest, down, up, truncate, with/without exceptions); used in quantization, graphics, DSP where precise rounding control is needed.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.round.ps
description: Rounds packed single-precision floats per imm8 mode (nearest, down, up, truncate, with/without exceptions); used in image/audio processing and SIMD quantization.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.round.sd
description: Rounds the low double-precision scalar per imm8 mode (passes through upper bits); used when rounding a single double with explicit rounding control.

name: llvm.x86.sse41.round.ss
description: Rounds the low single-precision scalar per imm8 mode (passes through upper bits); used when rounding a single float with explicit rounding control.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.crc32.32.16
description: Updates a 32-bit CRC-32C with a 16-bit chunk; used for fast checksums and hash updates over short data.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.crc32.32.32
description: Updates a 32-bit CRC-32C with a 32-bit chunk; used for accelerated checksums over 32-bit words.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.crc32.32.8
description: Updates a 32-bit CRC-32C with an 8-bit byte; used to stream CRC computation byte-by-byte.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.crc32.64.64
description: Updates a CRC-32C with a 64-bit chunk using a 64-bit accumulator (result in low 32 bits); used on x86-64 for high-throughput CRC over 64-bit data.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestri128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare (bytes/words, controlled by imm8) returning an index; used for SIMD strchr/strstr/strncmp-style searches.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestria128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare returning the AF flag; used to branch on comparison conditions without materializing masks.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestric128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare returning the CF flag; used for quick condition checks in text/scanning loops.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestrio128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare returning the OF flag; used to steer control flow based on compare outcome.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestris128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare returning the SF flag; used to branch on sign-style result semantics.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestriz128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare returning the ZF flag; used to test for “no matches”/“all matched” cases.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpestrm128
description: Explicit-length packed string compare producing a per-element 128-bit mask; used to build match bitmasks for further SIMD processing.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistri128
description: Implicit-length (null-terminated) packed string compare returning an index; used for SIMD scanning of C strings (e.g., strchr/memchr).

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistria128
description: Implicit-length packed string compare returning the AF flag; used for branching on string-compare conditions.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistric128
description: Implicit-length packed string compare returning the CF flag; used to quickly test comparison outcomes in parsers.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistrio128
description: Implicit-length packed string compare returning the OF flag; used to guide control flow based on match/mismatch status.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistris128
description: SSE4.2 PCMPISTRI: compares packed strings with implicit lengths; returns index of first match/mismatch and the S-flag test; useful for SIMD text search/classification (e.g., strchr/strstr, parsing).

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistriz128
description: SSE4.2 PCMPISTRI (implicit-length string compare); returns match/mismatch index and the Z-flag test (any match?); used in vectorized string ops and parsing.

name: llvm.x86.sse42.pcmpistrm128
description: SSE4.2 PCMPISTRM; compares packed chars/words per imm8 and returns a per-element mask vector; used to build match masks for SIMD string scanning.

name: llvm.x86.sse4a.extrq
description: AMD SSE4a EXTRQ (variable); extract a bitfield from the low 64 bits of an XMM, right-justified; fast bitfield extraction for parsers/codecs.

name: llvm.x86.sse4a.extrqi
description: AMD SSE4a EXTRQI (immediate); same as EXTRQ but bit position/length are immediates; bitfield extraction with compile-time offsets.

name: llvm.x86.sse4a.insertq
description: AMD SSE4a INSERTQ (variable); insert a bitfield into the low 64 bits of an XMM at a given position/length; building packed bitfields, headers.

name: llvm.x86.sse4a.insertqi
description: AMD SSE4a INSERTQI (immediate); same as INSERTQ with immediate position/length; efficient fixed-layout bitfield insertion.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pabs.b
description: SSSE3 per-element absolute value of 8-bit integers; used in SAD, image gradients, signal processing.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pabs.d
description: SSSE3 per-element absolute value of 32-bit integers; used in vector math, DSP, error metrics.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pabs.w
description: SSSE3 per-element absolute value of 16-bit integers; used in audio/image processing and SAD kernels.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phadd.d
description: SSSE3 horizontal add of 32-bit ints (MMX width); pairwise sums within lanes; used for partial reductions, dot products.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phadd.d.128
description: SSSE3 horizontal add of 32-bit ints (XMM 128-bit); pairwise sums; used for reductions and vector compaction.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phadd.sw
description: SSSE3 horizontal add of 16-bit ints with signed saturation; avoids overflow in audio mixing, image filters.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phadd.sw.128
description: SSSE3 horizontal add of 16-bit ints with signed saturation (128-bit); used in DSP/audio pipelines.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phadd.w
description: SSSE3 horizontal add of 16-bit ints (wrap-around); sums adjacent elements; used in reductions and convolution prep.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phadd.w.128
description: SSSE3 horizontal add of 16-bit ints (wrap-around, 128-bit); pairwise sums for SIMD reductions.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phsub.d
description: SSSE3 horizontal subtract of 32-bit ints (MMX width); pairwise differences; used in transforms and differential coding.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phsub.d.128
description: SSSE3 horizontal subtract of 32-bit ints (128-bit); pairwise differences; used in DCT/filters.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phsub.sw
description: SSSE3 horizontal subtract of 16-bit ints with signed saturation; used in image/audio differencing without overflow.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phsub.sw.128
description: SSSE3 horizontal subtract of 16-bit ints with signed saturation (128-bit); used in SIMD transforms and filters.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phsub.w
description: Horizontally subtract adjacent 16-bit integers in a 64-bit vector; useful for gradients, DCT/IDCT steps, and audio/image filtering.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.phsub.w.128
description: Horizontally subtract adjacent 16-bit integers in a 128-bit vector; useful for gradients, DCT/IDCT steps, and audio/image filtering.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pmadd.ub.sw
description: Multiply unsigned 8-bit by signed 8-bit, then add adjacent pairs into saturated 16-bit results; ideal for dot products, FIR filters, 8-bit quantized neural nets.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pmadd.ub.sw.128
description: 128-bit version of pmaddubsw; used for vectorized dot products, convolutions, and image/audio kernels.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pmul.hr.sw
description: Multiply 16-bit signed pairs and return rounded high 16 bits (Q15-style); used in fixed-point math, audio mixing, resampling, and image scaling.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pmul.hr.sw.128
description: 128-bit version of pmulhrsw; used in Q15/Q1.15 fixed-point pipelines for DSP and media.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pshuf.b
description: Byte-wise shuffle under per-byte control with optional zeroing (PSHUFB) in 64-bit vectors; great for swizzles, endianness fixes, nibble tables, and crypto S-box steps.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.pshuf.b.128
description: 128-bit PSHUFB; flexible byte permutes/zeroing for table lookups, pack/unpack, and bit-sliced crypto.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.psign.b
description: Conditionally negate/zero 8-bit elements based on the sign of a control vector; used for vectorized abs, sign application, and directional operations.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.psign.b.128
description: 128-bit version of PSIGNB; used for fast absolute values and sign-controlled arithmetic on bytes.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.psign.d
description: Conditionally negate/zero 32-bit elements based on control sign (PSIGND); useful for sign masks and branchless arithmetic.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.psign.d.128
description: 128-bit PSIGND; apply signs to 32-bit lanes without branches.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.psign.w
description: Conditionally negate/zero 16-bit elements based on control sign (PSIGNW); common in signal/image processing.

name: llvm.x86.ssse3.psign.w.128
description: 128-bit PSIGNW; efficient lane-wise sign application/abs on 16-bit data.

name: llvm.x86.sttilecfg
description: Store the current Intel AMX tile configuration to memory; used to save/restore AMX state across reconfiguration or context switches.

name: llvm.x86.stui
description: Enable user-mode interrupts (UINTR) by setting the UIE flag; used when a thread is ready to receive user interrupts.

name: llvm.x86.subborrow.32
description: 32-bit subtract with borrow-in, returning result and borrow-out; used for multi-precision/bignum subtraction.

name: llvm.x86.subborrow.64
description: 64-bit subtract with borrow-in, returning result and borrow-out; used for multi-precision/bignum subtraction.

name: llvm.x86.tbm.bextri.u32
description: Extract a bitfield into low bits based on start/length control (TBM BEXTRI); useful for parsing packed headers and bitfields.

name: llvm.x86.tbm.bextri.u64
description: 64-bit TBM BEXTRI bitfield extract into low bits; used for fast bitfield extraction and bit slicing.

name: llvm.x86.tcmmimfp16ps
description: AMX complex tile matmul: computes and accumulates the imaginary part from FP16 inputs into FP32 tile registers; used for complex GEMM in DSP/communications.

name: llvm.x86.tcmmimfp16ps.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tcmmimfp16ps; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tcmmrlfp16ps
description: AMX complex tile matmul: computes and accumulates the real part from FP16 inputs into FP32 tile registers; used for complex GEMM in DSP/communications.

name: llvm.x86.tcmmrlfp16ps.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tcmmrlfp16ps; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbf16ps
description: AMX BF16 tile dot-product, accumulating into FP32; used in BF16 GEMM/convolution for ML training/inference.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbf16ps.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tdpbf16ps; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbssd
description: AMX INT8 tile dot-product signed×signed, accumulating into int32; used in int8 GEMM/conv for inference.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbssd.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tdpbssd; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbsud
description: AMX INT8 tile dot-product signed×unsigned, accumulating into int32; used in int8 GEMM/conv for inference.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbsud.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tdpbsud; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbusd
description: AMX INT8 tile dot-product unsigned×signed, accumulating into int32; used in int8 GEMM/conv for inference.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbusd.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tdpbusd; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbuud
description: AMX INT8 tile dot-product unsigned×unsigned, accumulating into int32; used in int8 GEMM/conv for inference.

name: llvm.x86.tdpbuud.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tdpbuud; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tdpfp16ps
description: AMX FP16 tile dot-product, accumulating into FP32; used in FP16 GEMM/convolution for ML workloads.

name: llvm.x86.tdpfp16ps.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tdpfp16ps; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.testui
description: Tests for pending user interrupts (UINTR); used in low-latency runtimes or libraries to poll for user-level interrupts.

name: llvm.x86.tileloadd64
description: Loads a 2D tile from memory into a tile register with 64-bit row stride; used to stage matrix tiles for AMX compute in GEMM/conv.

name: llvm.x86.tileloadd64.internal
description: Same as llvm.x86.tileloadd64; internal LLVM codegen form, not for direct use.

name: llvm.x86.tileloaddt164
description: Loads a 2D tile with a “dt1” (L1-temporal) cache hint and 64-bit row stride; used when tile data will be reused soon in tiled kernels.

name: llvm.x86.tileloaddt164.internal
description: AMX: load a 2D tile from memory using the DT1 (temporal) cache hint; backend-only form with 64-bit addressing/stride; used for streaming tile loads in GEMM/conv kernels.

name: llvm.x86.tilerelease
description: AMX: release tile state (palette/config); called after AMX use to free HW resources and enable context switches.

name: llvm.x86.tilestored64
description: AMX: store a tile register to memory using the configured row bytes; 64-bit addressing; used to write back results of tiled GEMM/conv.

name: llvm.x86.tilestored64.internal
description: AMX: backend-only form of TILESTORED with 64-bit operands; same semantics; used by codegen for tiled stores.

name: llvm.x86.tilezero
description: AMX: zero a tile register; used to initialize accumulator/output tiles.

name: llvm.x86.tilezero.internal
description: AMX: backend-only form of TILEZERO; same semantics; used by codegen.

name: llvm.x86.tpause
description: WAITPKG: timed low-power pause until TSC deadline or interrupt; used in timed backoff/spin-wait loops to reduce power.

name: llvm.x86.umonitor
description: WAITPKG: set up a monitor on a user-space address; used before umwait for futex-like waits and efficient user-space blocking.

name: llvm.x86.umwait
description: WAITPKG: wait in low-power state until the monitored address is written or a timeout occurs; used for low-latency, low-power waits.

name: llvm.x86.urdmsr
description: Read a whitelisted MSR from user mode; OS-controlled; used for low-overhead access to performance/telemetry MSRs.

name: llvm.x86.uwrmsr
description: Write a whitelisted MSR from user mode; OS-controlled; used for low-latency updates to select MSRs (e.g., perf config).

name: llvm.x86.vbcstnebf162ps128
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: broadcast a scalar bfloat16 from memory and convert to 128-bit packed float32; used to quickly fill vectors from bf16 constants (ML workloads).

name: llvm.x86.vbcstnebf162ps256
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: broadcast a scalar bfloat16 from memory and convert to 256-bit packed float32; same use as above with wider vectors.

name: llvm.x86.vbcstnesh2ps128
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: broadcast a scalar FP16 (half) from memory and convert to 128-bit packed float32; useful for half-precision constants.

name: llvm.x86.vbcstnesh2ps256
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: broadcast a scalar FP16 (half) from memory and convert to 256-bit packed float32; wider-vector variant.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneebf162ps128
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: convert even-indexed lanes of packed bfloat16 to 128-bit float32; used with the odd variant to deinterleave/convert full bf16 vectors.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneebf162ps256
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: convert even-indexed lanes of packed bfloat16 to 256-bit float32; same use as above, wider.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneeph2ps128
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: convert even-indexed lanes of packed FP16 (half) to 128-bit float32; used when unpacking interleaved half-precision data.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneeph2ps256
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: convert even-indexed lanes of packed FP16 (half) to 256-bit float32; wider-vector variant.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneobf162ps128
description: AVX-NE-CONVERT: convert odd-indexed lanes of packed bfloat16 to 128-bit float32; paired with the even variant to cover all lanes.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneobf162ps256
description: Convert packed bfloat16 to packed single-precision floats (256-bit); used to dequantize BF16 tensors in ML and mixed-precision workloads.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneoph2ps128
description: Convert packed IEEE FP16 (half) to packed single-precision floats (128-bit); used when loading FP16 data for compute, graphics, and ML.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneoph2ps256
description: Convert packed IEEE FP16 (half) to packed single-precision floats (256-bit); used when loading FP16 data for compute, graphics, and ML.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneps2bf16128
description: Convert packed single-precision floats to bfloat16 with round-to-nearest-even (128-bit); used to downconvert activations/gradients to BF16 to save memory/bandwidth.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtneps2bf16256
description: Convert packed single-precision floats to bfloat16 with round-to-nearest-even (256-bit); used to downconvert activations/gradients to BF16 to save memory/bandwidth.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtps2ph.128
description: Convert packed single-precision floats to IEEE FP16 with selectable rounding (128-bit); used for storage/compression and mixed-precision inference.

name: llvm.x86.vcvtps2ph.256
description: Convert packed single-precision floats to IEEE FP16 with selectable rounding (256-bit); used for storage/compression and mixed-precision inference.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8affineinvqb.128
description: Per-byte GF(2^8) multiplicative inverse followed by affine transform (128-bit); used to implement AES S-box/inv S-box and similar substitutions without tables.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8affineinvqb.256
description: Per-byte GF(2^8) multiplicative inverse followed by affine transform (256-bit); used to implement AES S-box/inv S-box and similar substitutions without tables.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8affineinvqb.512
description: Per-byte GF(2^8) multiplicative inverse followed by affine transform (512-bit); used to implement AES S-box/inv S-box and similar substitutions without tables.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8affineqb.128
description: Per-byte affine transform over GF(2) (128-bit); used for AES linear steps, bitsliced transforms, scramblers.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8affineqb.256
description: Per-byte affine transform over GF(2) (256-bit); used for AES linear steps, bitsliced transforms, scramblers.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8affineqb.512
description: Per-byte affine transform over GF(2) (512-bit); used for AES linear steps, bitsliced transforms, scramblers.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8mulb.128
description: Per-byte multiplication in GF(2^8) with AES polynomial reduction (128-bit); used for AES MixColumns, Reed–Solomon/GF-based codes, GCM tables, RAID.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8mulb.256
description: Per-byte multiplication in GF(2^8) with AES polynomial reduction (256-bit); used for AES MixColumns, Reed–Solomon/GF-based codes, GCM tables, RAID.

name: llvm.x86.vgf2p8mulb.512
description: Per-byte multiplication in GF(2^8) with AES polynomial reduction (512-bit); used for AES MixColumns, Reed–Solomon/GF-based codes, GCM tables, RAID.

name: llvm.x86.vsha512msg1
description: Compute the first step of SHA-512 message schedule expansion; used to accelerate SHA-512 hashing.

name: llvm.x86.vsha512msg2
description: Compute the second step of SHA-512 message schedule expansion; used to accelerate SHA-512 hashing.

name: llvm.x86.vsha512rnds2
description: Perform two SHA-512 compression rounds on the state using message words/constants; used to speed up the SHA-512 core round function.

name: llvm.x86.vsm3msg1
description: Compute part of the SM3 message schedule expansion; used to accelerate the SM3 hash algorithm.

name: llvm.x86.vsm3msg2
description: Computes the SM3 message schedule (msg2 step) on vectors; used to accelerate SM3 hashing.

name: llvm.x86.vsm3rnds2
description: Performs two rounds of the SM3 compression function on vectors; speeds up SM3 hash computations.

name: llvm.x86.vsm4key4128
description: Generates 4 SM4 round keys (128-bit/XMM form); used in SM4 key schedule for vectorized crypto.

name: llvm.x86.vsm4key4256
description: Generates 4 SM4 round keys (256-bit/YMM form); used for parallel SM4 key schedule generation.

name: llvm.x86.vsm4rnds4128
description: Executes 4 rounds of the SM4 cipher (128-bit/XMM); accelerates SM4 encrypt/decrypt.

name: llvm.x86.vsm4rnds4256
description: Executes 4 rounds of the SM4 cipher (256-bit/YMM); enables parallel SM4 encrypt/decrypt.

name: llvm.x86.wbinvd
description: Writes back and invalidates all caches; used by OS/firmware before DMA, cache mode changes, or reset.

name: llvm.x86.wbnoinvd
description: Writes back modified cache lines without invalidation; used by OS/firmware for durability (e.g., NVRAM) with less disruption.

name: llvm.x86.wrfsbase.32
description: Writes lower 32 bits of FS base; used to set TLS base or during context switches in 32-bit environments.

name: llvm.x86.wrfsbase.64
description: Writes 64-bit FS base; used to set thread-local storage base or during context switches on x86-64.

name: llvm.x86.wrgsbase.32
description: Writes lower 32 bits of GS base; used for TLS/per-CPU base in 32-bit systems.

name: llvm.x86.wrgsbase.64
description: Writes 64-bit GS base; used for TLS/per-CPU base management on x86-64.

name: llvm.x86.wrpkru
description: Writes the PKRU register (Memory Protection Keys); used to change per-key user-mode access rights cheaply.

name: llvm.x86.wrssd
description: Writes a 32-bit value to the supervisor shadow stack (WRSS); used by OS to manage CET shadow stacks.

name: llvm.x86.wrssq
description: Writes a 64-bit value to the supervisor shadow stack (WRSS); used by OS/kernel for CET shadow stack maintenance.

name: llvm.x86.wrussd
description: Writes a 32-bit value to the user shadow stack (WRUSS); used by runtimes/signal handlers/JITs under CET.

name: llvm.x86.wrussq
description: Writes a 64-bit value to the user shadow stack (WRUSS); used to adjust user-mode shadow stacks under CET.

name: llvm.x86.xabort
description: Aborts a TSX RTM transaction with an 8-bit code; used for conditional rollback and fast-path fallback.

name: llvm.x86.xbegin
description: Begins a TSX RTM transaction; returns a status on abort; used for transactional critical sections/lock elision.

name: llvm.x86.xend
description: Ends (commits) a TSX RTM transaction; used to complete transactional critical sections.

name: llvm.x86.xgetbv
description: Reads an XCR (via XGETBV); used to query OS-enabled AVX/XSAVE features and state sizes during CPU feature probing.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vfrcz.pd
description: Fractional part of packed doubles (x − trunc(x)); used for range reduction in graphics/numerics.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vfrcz.pd.256
description: Fractional part of packed doubles in 256-bit vectors; range reduction for vector math/graphics.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vfrcz.ps
description: Fractional part of packed floats (x − trunc(x)); used for range reduction, shaders, noise.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vfrcz.ps.256
description: Fractional part of packed floats in 256-bit vectors; graphics/numeric range reduction.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vfrcz.sd
description: Fractional part of scalar double (x − trunc(x)); scalar range reduction.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vfrcz.ss
description: Fractional part of scalar float (x − trunc(x)); scalar range reduction.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpermil2pd
description: Two-source permute of packed doubles with mask/imm control; build shuffles/blends within lanes (swizzles, transposes).

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpermil2pd.256
description: 256-bit two-source permute of doubles with mask/imm; flexible shuffles/blends for vectorized code.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpermil2ps
description: Two-source permute of packed floats with mask/imm control; swizzles, transposes, complex-number ops.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpermil2ps.256
description: 256-bit two-source permute of floats with mask/imm; general data rearrangement/blending.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddbd
description: Horizontal add: groups of 4 signed bytes -> 32-bit sums; dot products, SAD/SSD, checksums.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddbq
description: Horizontal add: groups of 8 signed bytes -> 64-bit sums; reductions on byte data.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddbw
description: Horizontal add: adjacent signed bytes -> 16-bit sums; byte-pair reductions, image processing.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphadddq
description: Horizontal add: adjacent signed 32-bit ints -> 64-bit sums; integer reductions/accumulators.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddubd
description: Horizontal add: groups of 4 unsigned bytes -> 32-bit sums; dot products/SAD on unsigned pixels.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddubq
description: Horizontal add: groups of 8 unsigned bytes -> 64-bit sums; histogram/checksum-style reductions.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddubw
description: Horizontal add: adjacent unsigned bytes -> 16-bit sums; pixel pair sums, filtering.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddudq
description: Horizontal add: adjacent unsigned 32-bit ints -> 64-bit sums; wide accumulations without overflow.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphadduwd
description: Horizontal add: adjacent unsigned 16-bit ints -> 32-bit sums; image/audio accumulations, reductions.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphadduwq
description: Horizontal add of adjacent unsigned 16-bit words with widening to 64-bit; used for unsigned reductions/partial sums where large headroom is needed (e.g., summing pixel blocks).

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddwd
description: Horizontal add of adjacent 16-bit words to 32-bit; used for SIMD reductions and partial sums in integer pipelines.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphaddwq
description: Horizontal add of adjacent 16-bit words with widening to 64-bit; used when many terms are accumulated to avoid overflow.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphsubbw
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent 8-bit bytes into 16-bit; used for neighbor differences, gradients, and edge detection.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphsubdq
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent 32-bit dwords into 64-bit; used for delta/gradient calculations with wide range.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vphsubwd
description: Horizontal subtract of adjacent 16-bit words into 32-bit; used for difference-of-neighbor operations.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacsdd
description: Packed signed 32×32 multiply-accumulate into 32-bit sums (wrap); used for 32-bit integer dot products, FIR/IIR filters.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacsdqh
description: Packed signed 32×32 multiply, accumulate into the high 32 bits of 64-bit lanes; used for wide-precision MACs/dot products.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacsdql
description: Packed signed 32×32 multiply, accumulate into the low 32 bits of 64-bit lanes; used for wide-precision MACs/dot products.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacssdd
description: Packed signed 32×32 multiply-accumulate into 32-bit with saturation; used in fixed-point MAC for audio/image.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacssdqh
description: Packed signed 32×32->64 MAC accumulating into high 32 bits with saturation; used for saturated wide-precision accumulation.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacssdql
description: Packed signed 32×32->64 MAC accumulating into low 32 bits with saturation; used for saturated wide-precision accumulation.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacsswd
description: Packed signed 16×16->32 multiply-accumulate with saturation; used for fixed-point filters and image/audio kernels.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacssww
description: Packed signed 16×16->16 multiply-accumulate with saturation; used for narrow-precision MAC with clipping (e.g., audio mixing).

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacswd
description: Packed signed 16×16->32 multiply-accumulate (wrap); used for integer dot products and convolutions.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmacsww
description: Packed signed 16×16->16 multiply-accumulate (wrap); used for compact/narrow accumulations.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmadcsswd
description: Packed signed 16×16 pairwise multiply-add into 32-bit with saturation; used for saturated 16-bit dot products into 32-bit.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpmadcswd
description: Packed signed 16×16 pairwise multiply-add into 32-bit (wrap); used for fast 16-bit dot products into 32-bit.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpperm
description: Byte-wise arbitrary permute/merge from two vectors under a control vector (can also emit 0x00/0xFF); used for table lookups, shuffles, packing/unpacking.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshab
description: Per-element bidirectional arithmetic shift of 8-bit lanes by signed counts (left if positive, right arithmetic if negative); used for fixed-point scaling and bit-field ops on bytes.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshad
description: Per-lane bidirectional arithmetic shift for 32-bit ints; shift counts come from a vector, negative = left, positive = arithmetic right; useful for SIMD bit math and crypto.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshaq
description: Per-lane bidirectional arithmetic shift for 64-bit ints; counts from a vector, negative = left, positive = arithmetic right; useful for SIMD bit manipulation and hashing.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshaw
description: Per-lane bidirectional arithmetic shift for 16-bit ints; counts from a vector, negative = left, positive = arithmetic right; useful in packed-signed arithmetic and signal processing.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshlb
description: Per-lane bidirectional logical shift for 8-bit ints; counts from a vector, negative = left, positive = logical right; used in SIMD bit twiddling and data alignment.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshld
description: Per-lane bidirectional logical shift for 32-bit ints; counts from a vector, negative = left, positive = logical right; used for SIMD masks, bitfields, and codecs.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshlq
description: Per-lane bidirectional logical shift for 64-bit ints; counts from a vector, negative = left, positive = logical right; used in hashing and wide-bit operations.

name: llvm.x86.xop.vpshlw
description: Per-lane bidirectional logical shift for 16-bit ints; counts from a vector, negative = left, positive = logical right; used in image and audio SIMD kernels.

name: llvm.x86.xresldtrk
description: Resume load-address tracking (XRESLDTRK); paired with XSUSLDTRK to control TSX load tracking for security mitigations and tuned transactional regions in OS runtimes/JITs.

name: llvm.x86.xrstor
description: Restore processor extended state from an XSAVE area (XRSTOR); used in context switches, signal handlers, and VMMs.

name: llvm.x86.xrstor64
description: 64-bit addressing variant of XRSTOR; same use cases in 64-bit kernels and hypervisors.

name: llvm.x86.xrstors
description: Restore user and supervisor extended states from an XSAVES-format area (XRSTORS); used by OS/VMM when supervisor states are enabled.

name: llvm.x86.xrstors64
description: 64-bit addressing variant of XRSTORS; used for restoring XSAVES areas in 64-bit environments.

name: llvm.x86.xsave
description: Save processor extended state components selected by XCR0 to memory (XSAVE); used for thread context switch and lazy FPU/SIMD state management.

name: llvm.x86.xsave64
description: 64-bit addressing variant of XSAVE; used by 64-bit kernels and hypervisors for state save.

name: llvm.x86.xsavec
description: Save extended state in compacted format (XSAVEC) to reduce footprint; used to minimize XSAVE area size in OS/VMM context management.

name: llvm.x86.xsavec64
description: 64-bit addressing variant of XSAVEC; compact saves in 64-bit environments.

name: llvm.x86.xsaveopt
description: Optimized XSAVE that may skip unchanged components (XSAVEOPT); reduces save overhead in frequent context switches.

name: llvm.x86.xsaveopt64
description: 64-bit addressing variant of XSAVEOPT; optimized state saves in 64-bit systems.

name: llvm.x86.xsaves
description: Save user and supervisor states using XSAVES format, governed by XCR0 and IA32_XSS; used by OS/VMMs managing CET, PT, and other supervisor features.

name: llvm.x86.xsaves64
description: 64-bit addressing variant of XSAVES; supervisor-aware compact saves in 64-bit kernels and hypervisors.

name: llvm.x86.xsetbv
description: Writes an x86 extended control register (XCR) via XSETBV; used by OS kernels/hypervisors to enable/disable XSAVE-managed states (e.g., SSE, AVX, AVX‑512, AMX) during boot and context management.

name: llvm.x86.xsusldtrk
description: Suspends hardware load-address tracking (XSUSLDTRK); used by low-level OS/firmware to temporarily disable load tracking around sensitive regions, then re-enabled with XRESLDTRK.

name: llvm.x86.xtest
description: Tests whether the CPU is currently executing inside a TSX (RTM/HLE) transaction; used in lock elision and transactional memory code to pick transactional vs fallback paths.
